---
title: "Analytical PK Models"
---

ferx-core provides closed-form analytical solutions for one-, two-, and
three-compartment PK models. These are the preferred structural model type:
they are faster than ODE integration and support the Enzyme automatic
differentiation path for gradient computation.

## Invoking a model in the DSL

The `[structural_model]` section of a `.ferx` file selects an analytical model
with the `pk` keyword:

```
[structural_model]
  pk one_cpt_oral(cl=CL, v=V, ka=KA)
```

Named arguments map individual-parameter symbols to the model's PK slots. The
available models and their required arguments are listed below.

## One-compartment models

All one-compartment solutions are in `pk/one_compartment.rs`.

### IV bolus

```
pk one_cpt_iv_bolus(cl=CL, v=V)
```

$$C(t) = \frac{\text{Dose}}{V} \cdot e^{-k \cdot t}, \quad k = \frac{CL}{V}$$

### IV infusion

```
pk one_cpt_iv_infusion(cl=CL, v=V)
```

During infusion ($t \le T_{\text{inf}}$):
$$C(t) = \frac{\text{Rate}}{CL} \cdot (1 - e^{-k t})$$

After infusion ($t > T_{\text{inf}}$):
$$C(t) = \frac{\text{Rate}}{CL} \cdot (1 - e^{-k T_{\text{inf}}}) \cdot e^{-k(t - T_{\text{inf}})}$$

### Oral absorption

```
pk one_cpt_oral(cl=CL, v=V, ka=KA)
pk one_cpt_oral_f(cl=CL, v=V, ka=KA, f=F)   # with bioavailability
```

$$C(t) = \frac{F \cdot \text{Dose} \cdot K_A}{V (K_A - k)} \left(e^{-k t} - e^{-K_A t}\right)$$

When $K_A \approx k$ (flip-flop, within `1e-6`), the L'Hôpital limit is used
to avoid catastrophic cancellation:

$$C(t) = \frac{F \cdot \text{Dose} \cdot K_A}{V} \cdot t \cdot e^{-k t}$$

## Two-compartment models

All two-compartment solutions are in `pk/two_compartment.rs`.

Macro rate constants are derived from micro-constants using Vieta's formula to
avoid subtracting two nearly-equal large numbers when $s \gg \sqrt{s^2 - 4d}$:

```rust
let alpha = (s + disc) / 2.0;
let beta  = d / alpha;           // Vieta: α·β = d
```

where $s = k_{10} + k_{12} + k_{21}$ and $d = k_{10} \cdot k_{21}$.

### IV bolus

```
pk two_cpt_iv_bolus(cl=CL, v1=V1, q=Q, v2=V2)
```

$$C(t) = A e^{-\alpha t} + B e^{-\beta t}$$

$$A = \frac{\text{Dose}}{V_1} \cdot \frac{\alpha - k_{21}}{\alpha - \beta}, \quad B = \frac{\text{Dose}}{V_1} \cdot \frac{k_{21} - \beta}{\alpha - \beta}$$

### IV infusion

```
pk two_cpt_iv_infusion(cl=CL, v1=V1, q=Q, v2=V2)
```

Superposition of the bolus solution across the infusion duration; switches to
bolus formula when `duration ≤ 0`.

### Oral absorption

```
pk two_cpt_oral(cl=CL, v1=V1, q=Q, v2=V2, ka=KA)
pk two_cpt_oral_f(cl=CL, v1=V1, q=Q, v2=V2, ka=KA, f=F)
```

$$C(t) = Ae^{-\alpha t} + Be^{-\beta t} + Ce^{-K_A t}$$

Coefficients derived analytically; degenerate cases ($K_A \approx \alpha$ or
$K_A \approx \beta$) handled via L'Hôpital limits.

## Three-compartment models

Three-compartment solutions are in `pk/three_compartment.rs`. Macro rate
constants ($\alpha$, $\beta$, $\gamma$) are solved from the characteristic
polynomial using Cardano's formula.

```
pk three_cpt_iv_bolus(cl=CL, v1=V1, q2=Q2, v2=V2, q3=Q3, v3=V3)
pk three_cpt_iv_infusion(cl=CL, v1=V1, q2=Q2, v2=V2, q3=Q3, v3=V3)
pk three_cpt_oral(cl=CL, v1=V1, q2=Q2, v2=V2, q3=Q3, v3=V3, ka=KA)
```

## Multi-dose superposition

All analytical models support multiple dose events. The `pk/mod.rs` dispatcher
calls the single-dose solution for each historical dose and sums the
contributions at each observation time:

```rust
// Conceptual superposition
for dose in &subject.doses {
    let t_since_dose = t_obs - dose.time;
    if t_since_dose >= 0.0 {
        pred += single_dose_solution(dose, t_since_dose, &pk_params);
    }
}
```

## `PkParams` layout

PK parameters are passed through a fixed-layout `[f64; 8]` array rather than a
`HashMap` so that Enzyme's reverse-mode AD can differentiate through them:

| Index | Symbol | Description |
|---|---|---|
| 0 | `CL` | Clearance |
| 1 | `V` / `V1` | Volume (central) |
| 2 | `Q` | Intercompartmental clearance (central ↔ peripheral 1) |
| 3 | `V2` | Peripheral volume 1 |
| 4 | `KA` | Oral absorption rate constant |
| 5 | `F` | Bioavailability (default 1.0) |
| 6 | `Q3` | Intercompartmental clearance (central ↔ peripheral 2; 3-cmt) |
| 7 | `V3` | Peripheral volume 2 (3-cmt) |

Unused slots default to 0.0 (except F, which defaults to 1.0).

## Relevant source files

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `pk/one_compartment.rs` | 1-cpt IV bolus, infusion, oral, oral-with-F |
| `pk/two_compartment.rs` | 2-cpt IV bolus, infusion, oral, oral-with-F; macro rate constants |
| `pk/three_compartment.rs` | 3-cpt IV bolus and infusion; Cardano characteristic roots |
| `pk/mod.rs` | `compute_predictions()`, multi-dose superposition, TV-covariate dispatch |
| `types.rs` | `PkParams` layout constants (`PK_IDX_CL`, `PK_IDX_V`, …) |
